run.notes: One evidence-backed persistence signal was promoted.

revision.title: Dream state moves into a durable ledger
revision.summary: Dream is adopting durable state rather than ephemeral summaries.
revision.delta: A Signal and Topic ledger is now part of the architecture.
revision.mechanism: A transactional store owns stable identities and append-only revisions.
revision.consequence: Trend judgments can be revised and de-duplicated across days.
revision.evidence_boundary: The supplied evidence establishes design adoption, not production scale.

facts.claim: The commit and focused tests establish a persistent ledger.

inferences: Stable identity should reduce duplicate daily findings.
unknowns: Production contention is not yet observed.
next_checks: Verify a repeated horizon creates no duplicate Signal.

forecasts.claim: Repeated input will update or suppress this Signal rather than recreate it.
expected_observations: A duplicate-only or update run references this Signal ID.

topic_decision.reason: The persistence mechanism has durable identity, transaction, and revision semantics worth explaining.

revision.title: Append-only repository intelligence state
revision.summary: How stable identity, revisions, and transactions turn reports into revisable knowledge.
revision.thesis: The ledger separates current projections from immutable reasoning history.
revision.mechanism: Stable UUID identity, fingerprint aliases, append-only revisions, and cursor-last transactions.
revision.applicability: Daily repository intelligence where repeated observations overlap.
boundaries: Does not prove semantic duplicate retrieval at unlimited scale.

findings.claim: Durable identity is distinct from an evolving fingerprint.

engineering_implications: Cursor movement must be coupled to the same transaction as revisions.
